chief_complaint: A 50-year-old woman presented with acute left limb weakness and slurred speech.
history: The patient has a history of hyperthyroidism and paroxysmal atrial fibrillation without regular treatment. She presented with acute symptoms 78 minutes after onset.
exam: Vital signs: not provided. Physical examination revealed left limb weakness and slurred speech.
labs: ECG: AF with a rapid ventricular rate of 165 beats per minute. Chest radiography: cardiomegaly with bilateral pleural effusion. Laboratory tests: elevated free thyroxine, triiodothyronine, antithyroid-stimulating hormone (TSH) receptor antibody, and reduced TSH content. Echocardiography: global left ventricular hypokinesia.
